metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_5195
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1: RPS23RG1 knockout mice exhibit learning and memory deficits and synaptic plasticity impairment.

Figure 2: postsynapse maturation and synapse density was impaired in RPS23RG1 knockout mice.

Figure 3, 4: RPS23RG1 knockout enhanced PSD93 (DLG2) and PSD95 (DLG4) turnover.

Figure 4c, S7, S7: pulldown of endogenous RPS23RG1 in wildtype and RPS23RG1-knockout mice showed interaction with PSD93 (DLG2) and PSD95 (DLG4), competing for binding with MDM2 and thus attenuating ubiquitination.

Figure 4d: confocal imaging of overexpressed human HA-tagged RPS23RG1 in mouse primary neuronal cultures showed partial co-localization with PSD93 (DLG2) and PSD95 (DLG4).. Experimental description: annotated the confocal localization evidence in Figure 4d; "HA-tagged human RR1 was transfected into WT mouse primary neurons at DIV4."
